author: zlipeng
abstract: Save and load durable knowledge — project facts, conversation context, and user requirements/preferences — in a per-project memory store under ~/.agents/memories/. Each project gets its own subdirectory, named by encoding the absolute project root path the same way Claude Code names ~/.claude/projects/ directories (non-alphanumeric characters replaced by '-'). Saving is a confirm-before-write flow: the skill first reports exactly what it would persist (target paths, new vs. update, type, full content, index changes) and writes only after the user approves, so inaccurate content never lands. On save, an index.md table of contents is generated first and each fact is stored as a semantically-named <topic>-<detail>.md file with light YAML frontmatter classifying it as user / feedback / project / reference. On load, the index is read first for a cheap overview, then the relevant memory files are opened, applied, and briefly summarized. Triggered by direct invocation or instructions like 'save memory' / '保存记忆' / 'remember this' (save) and 'load project memory' / '读取该项目记忆' / 'what do you remember' (load).
